original_hypothesis.title: Unbounded Proxy Deployment Leads to Gas Exhaustion
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The owner can deploy an unlimited number of `LiquidProxy` contracts. Since functions like `getTotalStaked()` and `getTotalRewards()` iterate over all deployed proxies, deploying too many proxies will cause these view functions to exceed the block gas limit. This would permanently brick the `totalAssets()` calculation, disabling all core protocol functions and locking user funds.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: While owner-driven, this represents a permanent unrecoverable state if triggered accidentally or maliciously.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Pushes to stakingProxies without upper bound

preconditions: Owner deploys a large number of staking proxies

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: O(P * V) iteration where P is proxy count and V is validator count
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Protocol becomes unusable, locking all funds

economic_questions: How many proxies are required to hit the gas limit?
Is there any way to remove or disable a proxy?
validation_requirements: Measure gas consumed per proxy in totalAssets
Verify absence of proxy removal functionality

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
